articles.title: BBVA and OpenAI collaborate to transform global banking
articles.summary: BBVA has partnered with OpenAI to revolutionize the banking industry through the integration of artificial intelligence, aiming to enhance customer experiences and improve operational efficiency. This collaboration is expected to bring about significant innovations in areas such as chatbots, fraud detection, and personalized financial services. By leveraging OpenAI's cutting-edge technology, BBVA seeks to transform the future of global banking and stay ahead of the curve in the digital landscape.

articles.title: BNY builds “AI for everyone, everywhere” with OpenAI
articles.summary: BNY Mellon has partnered with OpenAI to develop an "AI for everyone, everywhere" platform, aiming to make artificial intelligence more accessible and widespread. This collaboration is expected to drive innovation and improve customer experiences across various industries. By leveraging OpenAI's technology, BNY Mellon seeks to democratize AI adoption and create new opportunities for growth and development.

articles.title: How We Used Codex to Ship Sora for Android in 28 Days
articles.summary: The development team behind Sora utilized Codex, a cutting-edge AI-powered coding tool, to successfully launch the Android version of their app in just 28 days. Codex's automated coding capabilities significantly accelerated the development process, enabling the team to meet their tight deadline. This achievement demonstrates the potential of AI-driven coding tools to revolutionize the speed and efficiency of software development.

articles.title: Advancing science and math with GPT-5.2
articles.summary: Researchers have unveiled GPT-5.2, a cutting-edge AI model that promises to revolutionize science and math education by providing personalized learning experiences and enhancing problem-solving skills. This innovative technology has the potential to make complex concepts more accessible and engaging for students. With GPT-5.2, the boundaries of human knowledge and understanding in science and math may be significantly expanded.

articles.title: Update to GPT-5 System Card: GPT-5.2
articles.summary: OpenAI has released GPT-5.2, an update to its GPT-5 system, which enhances the performance and capabilities of its language model. This upgrade is expected to improve the accuracy and coherence of generated text, further advancing the state-of-the-art in natural language processing. The GPT-5.2 update demonstrates OpenAI's ongoing commitment to refining its AI technology and pushing the boundaries of language understanding.

articles.title: Improved Gemini audio models for powerful voice experiences
articles.summary: Google has upgraded its Gemini audio models, enabling more accurate and efficient voice recognition for a wide range of applications. This improvement allows for better voice experiences, including enhanced speech-to-text capabilities and more accurate voice assistants. The updated models are expected to power innovative voice-based features in various devices and services.

articles.title: Deepening our partnership with the UK AI Security Institute
articles.summary: A new partnership has been formed with the UK AI Security Institute to advance AI security research and development. This collaboration aims to improve the safety and reliability of AI systems, addressing emerging security threats. By combining expertise, the partnership seeks to drive innovation and establish best practices in AI security.

articles.title: Strengthening our partnership with the UK government to support prosperity and security in the AI era
articles.summary: The UK government has partnered with a tech company to harness the power of AI for prosperity and security. This collaboration aims to drive innovation, create jobs, and enhance national security through AI-driven solutions. By working together, they seek to establish the UK as a leader in the responsible development and deployment of AI technologies.

articles.title: FACTS Benchmark Suite: Systematically evaluating the factuality of large language models
articles.summary: Researchers have introduced the FACTS Benchmark Suite, a comprehensive evaluation tool to assess the factuality of large language models, helping to identify and mitigate the spread of misinformation. This suite systematically tests language models' ability to distinguish between true and false statements, promoting more accurate and trustworthy AI-generated content. By using FACTS, developers can refine their models to produce more reliable and factual outputs.

articles.title: Engineering more resilient crops for a warming climate
articles.summary: Scientists are developing more resilient crops to withstand the challenges of a warming climate, using innovative engineering techniques to enhance their ability to thrive in extreme temperatures and weather conditions. This breakthrough has the potential to improve global food security and mitigate the impacts of climate change on agriculture. By creating climate-resilient crops, researchers aim to ensure sustainable food production for future generations.

articles.title: CUGA on Hugging Face: Democratizing Configurable AI Agents
articles.summary: Hugging Face has introduced CUGA, a platform that democratizes the creation of configurable AI agents, allowing users to easily build and customize their own AI models. This innovation makes AI more accessible to non-experts, enabling a wider range of individuals and organizations to harness the power of AI. By simplifying the development process, CUGA has the potential to accelerate AI adoption and drive new applications across various industries.

articles.title: Nemotron 3 Nano \- A new Standard for Efficient, Open, and Intelligent Agentic Models
articles.summary: The Nemotron 3 Nano introduces a new standard for agentic models, prioritizing efficiency, openness, and intelligence. This innovation enables more effective and adaptable models, potentially revolutionizing fields like AI and machine learning. By promoting open and collaborative development, the Nemotron 3 Nano aims to accelerate breakthroughs in intelligent systems.

articles.title: New in llama.cpp: Model Management
articles.summary: The latest update to llama.cpp introduces model management, allowing users to easily switch between different AI models and fine-tune their performance. This innovation enables more flexibility and customization in AI applications, making it simpler to adapt to various use cases. By streamlining model management, llama.cpp enhances the overall user experience and expands its potential applications.

articles.title: Codex is Open Sourcing AI models
articles.summary: Codex, a cutting-edge AI model, is being open-sourced, allowing developers to access and build upon its capabilities. This move is expected to accelerate innovation in the field of artificial intelligence and enable the creation of more sophisticated AI-powered tools. By making its models openly available, Codex is democratizing access to AI technology and fostering a community-driven approach to AI development.

articles.title: Apriel-1.6-15b-Thinker: Cost-efficient Frontier Multimodal Performance
articles.summary: Researchers have introduced Apriel-1.6-15b-Thinker, a cost-efficient AI model that achieves frontier multimodal performance, enabling efficient processing of various data types such as text, images, and audio. This innovation has the potential to improve applications like chatbots, virtual assistants, and content generation. By reducing computational costs, Apriel-1.6-15b-Thinker makes multimodal AI more accessible and widely adoptable.

articles.title: JetBlue flight averts mid-air collision with US Air Force jet
articles.summary: A JetBlue flight narrowly avoided a mid-air collision with a US Air Force jet, thanks to the quick response of air traffic controllers who alerted the pilots to the potential danger. The incident highlights the importance of effective communication and collision avoidance systems in preventing accidents. The close call is under investigation by the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to determine the cause and implement measures to prevent similar incidents.

articles.title: Secret Documents Show Pepsi and Walmart Colluded to Raise Food Prices
articles.summary: Leaked documents have revealed a shocking collusion between Pepsi and Walmart to artificially inflate food prices, sparking concerns over anti-competitive practices and potential harm to consumers. The secret documents, uncovered through a whistleblower, suggest the two companies worked together to manipulate prices and maximize profits. This bombshell revelation could have significant implications for the food industry and antitrust regulations.

articles.title: A quarter of US-trained scientists eventually leave
articles.summary: A significant proportion of scientists trained in the US, approximately 25%, ultimately leave the country to pursue careers abroad. This brain drain is attributed to various factors, including limited job opportunities and stricter immigration policies. The exodus of skilled scientists may have long-term implications for the US's competitiveness in the global scientific community.

articles.title: I'm Kenyan. I don't write like ChatGPT, ChatGPT writes like me
articles.summary: A Kenyan writer has pointed out that ChatGPT's writing style is often compared to that of humans, but in reality, the AI model has been trained on vast amounts of human-generated text, including African writers. This highlights the importance of diverse representation in AI training data, as ChatGPT's capabilities are a reflection of the collective writing styles of humans, including those from underrepresented regions. By acknowledging this, we can work towards creating more inclusive and culturally sensitive AI models.

articles.title: It seems that OpenAI is scraping [certificate transparency] logs
articles.summary: OpenAI has been found to be scraping Certificate Transparency (CT) logs, a public database of SSL/TLS certificates, potentially to gather website data and improve its AI models. This move has sparked debate among security experts and developers, raising concerns about data privacy and the ethics of web scraping. The scraping effort could significantly enhance OpenAI's language understanding capabilities, but also poses questions about the responsible use of publicly available data.
